page: Google Cloud Platform n2d-highmem-2 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance based on the x86_64 architecture. It features 2 vCPUs mapped to a single physical core with 2 threads per core, utilizing dedicated CPU allocation. The underlying processor is an AMD EPYC 7B13 with a 32 MB L3 cache, 0.5 MB L2 cache, and 32 KB L1d cache. The instance is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. It does not include local storage or bundled GPU hardware, and provides zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark results indicate a divergence between single-core and multi-core performance. The single-core CPU performance via stress-ng is strong, ranking in the top 25% with a score of 2678.19, while the multi-core CPU performance is weak, falling into the bottom 25% at 2685.18. Memory bandwidth tests show weak to poor performance, with read-only large block bandwidth at 31.35 GB/sec and write large block bandwidth at 22.01 GB/sec. Single-threaded compression and decompression tasks perform at average levels, with gzip compression reaching 25.75 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression at 11.1 GB/sec. However, complex multi-threaded workloads, such as software compilation, ray tracing, and database operations, score in the bottom 10% of benchmarks.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the n2d-highmem-2 offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio on dedicated AMD EPYC hardware, making it a viable option for memory-bound workloads that do not require high parallel processing power. Due to the limited 2 vCPU configuration and poor multi-core scaling, it is not suited for intensive compute, database operations, or high-throughput web serving. Instead, it is best aligned with lightweight memory-intensive applications, single-threaded data processing, and basic caching services where memory capacity is prioritized over raw computational throughput.
description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-highmem-2 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 2 dedicated vCPUs from an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor and 16.0 GB of RAM. This configuration provides a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core, though it lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks reveal strong single-core CPU capabilities, ranking in the top 25% in stress-ng tests, contrasted with weak multi-core performance and poor memory bandwidth. Single-threaded tasks like gzip compression and bzip3 decompression perform at average levels, whereas database operations, software compilation, and multi-core processing fall into the bottom 10%. Consequently, this instance is best suited for lightweight, memory-intensive workloads, single-threaded applications, and basic caching tasks that benefit from dedicated memory allocation rather than high parallel processing power.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-highmem-2 is a memory-optimized instance with 2 vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7B13 CPU, offering strong single-core performance for memory-bound, single-threaded workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-highmem-2 features 2 vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC CPU, optimized for lightweight memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processing and a high memory-to-vCPU ratio for single-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated AMD EPYC 7B13 processor with 2 vCPUs and 16 GB RAM
High memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core
Strong single-core CPU performance ranking in the top 25%
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Best suited for lightweight memory-bound and single-threaded applications
